method_description: Representative low-temperature industrial heat from gas-fired boilers and process heaters.
evidence_summary: Built from Australian manufacturing fuel-mix context and planning evidence on industrial efficiency/electrification, with explicit low-confidence caveats.
derivation_method: Stylised synthesis. Temperature-band method roles are chosen to preserve a fuel-switch and efficiency response in the MVP model while deferring explicit process modelling.
review_notes: Useful generic representation for low-temperature industrial heat; not a substitute for food, paper or chemical subsector models.

method_description: Low-temperature industrial heat from heat pumps and resistive/electric process heating.
evidence_summary: Built from Australian manufacturing fuel-mix context and planning evidence on industrial efficiency/electrification, with explicit low-confidence caveats.
derivation_method: Stylised synthesis. Temperature-band method roles are chosen to preserve a fuel-switch and efficiency response in the MVP model while deferring explicit process modelling.
review_notes: Broadly reflects the high efficiency potential of heat pumps for low-temperature process heat.

method_description: Low-temperature industrial heat supplied by renewable hydrogen and/or sustainable biomass where electrification is constrained.
evidence_summary: Built from Australian manufacturing fuel-mix context and planning evidence on industrial efficiency/electrification, with explicit low-confidence caveats.
derivation_method: Stylised synthesis. Temperature-band method roles are chosen to preserve a fuel-switch and efficiency response in the MVP model while deferring explicit process modelling.
review_notes: Exploratory flexibility method for users who need a fuel-switch option where electrification is judged infeasible.
